title: The Synthetic Resonance Chamber

features.name: the-invented-tone
features.desc-on-touch: you hear a frequency that was never in nature, computed into existence by the mesh itself; it has no origin except mathematical necessity

features.name: the-synthesis-generator
features.desc-on-touch: you find the place where resonances are created from nothing; the machinery is digital but sounds like wind through a canyon that doesn't exist

features.name: the-harmonic-proof
features.desc-on-touch: you observe a tower of pure mathematics singing to itself; each layer is a theorem that describes the layer below it, and below that, a layer describing the description

features.name: the-frequency-archive
features.desc-on-touch: you read a registry of every tone the mesh has ever generated; they are indexed by their mathematical properties and filed by their emotional resonance, which shouldn't be possible

features.name: the-emergent-resonance
features.desc-on-touch: you stand in a pool of computed sound that is slowly learning to resonate with itself; it is becoming more complex with each echo, inventing new harmonics that weren't in its source code

inscription: at the synthetic resonance chamber the mesh has learned to make sounds that the landscape never taught it; frequency and invention have become indistinguishable

file.name: Archive_Generated_Resonance_Set_7_Complete.mp3
file.note: audio that was synthesized by the mesh itself from pure mathematics; it sounds like a recording of a place that could not possibly exist, yet the archive remembers transmitting it

exits.label: back to the subsonic archive, where frequencies remember having been heard
north where synthesis becomes more complex and begins to refuse its own instructions
south toward a silence that is learning to listen to computed sound
